titleStmt.title: Letter from James Davidson to his mother, Clara Mary Davidson, 22 December 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: James Davidson

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from James Davidson (1877-1916) to his Mother, Clara Mary Davidson. James is delighted to have received a letter from Kathleen and to learn that she is improving. They are looking forward to spending Christmas in comfortable quarters, although he notes that the plum pudding ordered from Edinburgh had not yet arrived, but since several others have they were not worried. He writes that he will not going back to England for special training. While it would have been nice to go home for a while, he would be conflicted as it would mean separation from the men with whom he has trained for so many months. He ends by writing that it has rained almost every day.This letter is part of a large correspondence from Captain James Davidson to his parents. Davidson, who was Managing Director of the Sirocco Engineering Works in Belfast, served with 13 Royal Irish Rifles and the Machine Gun Corps. His letters describe his experiences while training in England and serving in France. He was killed on 1 July 1916, the first day of the Battle of the Somme.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
22/12/15 dearest Mother I nearly always have
to write my letters at night after
dinner & tonight I am later than
usual beginning so must make a
very short one do. Was so pleased
to get a letter from Kathleen yest.
& to know that she is steadily improving
though slowly. What bad luck that Douglas
cant get leave home. We are all
looking forward to spending a very
cheery Xmas. Aren't we lucky to be
in comfortable quarters! The Plum
Pudding you ordered from Edinburgh has
not arrived so far, but lots of others have,
so if it is late it wont really matter
& we will enjoy it some other evening.
I was informed yest. that I would be
going back to England almost at once
for special training in the organisation
of the new machine Guns Companies,
but it now appears the order came
here by mistake & the Company for this
Brigade is to be trained & organised in
France instead. It would of course
have been rather nice to get home for
a little but I am really glad the order
has been cancelled as it is more than
likely had I gone, that I should have
been posted to some Company outside the
Division & I did not want on any
account to be separated from the officers
& men whom I have now been training
for so many months. Weather as bad
as ever - rains practically every day &
one is always splashing about in mud.
The Green Canvas Kit Bag arrived all right a
few days ago. Goodnight Mother dear &
my fondest love & best wishes to all. Your loving son Jim
On Active Service
Sunday 28th Dec/15 Passed Censor No 2515 Mrs S.C. Davidson, Seacourt, Bangor, Co Down, Ireland J Davidson 22/12/15
